claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to ask you some questions about your child (or children).

**First question: How old is your child?**

This is important because:
- Children **under 16** automatically qualify (subject to other conditions)
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** do not qualify for Child Benefit

If you have multiple children, we can check each one - just let me know the age of the child you'd like to start with.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - Alex, who is 18 years old. I wanted to check if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let's check Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Since Alex is 18 years old, they fall into the **16-19 age category**. For children in this age group, eligibility depends on whether they're in approved education or training.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in education or training?**

This includes:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQ Level 3 or below)
- Approved vocational training

Or alternatively:
- Has Alex recently left education or training? (If they left within the last 20 weeks, you might still be eligible for an extension)

Please let me know which situation applies to Alex.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. They finished school a while back and aren't studying anymore.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Alex is not currently in education or training, I need to check one more thing:

**When did Alex finish their education or training?** 

Specifically, was it within the last 20 weeks? If so, you might still be eligible for an extension period of Child Benefit.

If it was longer than 20 weeks ago, then unfortunately Alex would not qualify as a "qualifying young person" for Child Benefit purposes.

Can you tell me approximately when Alex left education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It was quite a while ago now - definitely more than 20 weeks. They left school over a year ago actually.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me navigate to the appropriate outcome based on what you've told me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Decision: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Why?

For a child aged 16-19 to qualify for Child Benefit, they must be in **approved education or training**. This includes:
- Full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQ Level 3 or below)
- Approved vocational training

Since Alex is 18, not currently in education or training, and left school over a year ago (well beyond the 20-week extension period), they do not meet the criteria as a "qualifying young person."

### Important to know:

- Child Benefit automatically covers children **under 16**
- For those aged **16-19**, they must remain in approved education or training
- If a young person leaves education, there's a **20-week extension period** available, but only if they left recently and registered with a careers service or the armed services
- Once a child turns **20**, Child Benefit stops regardless of their circumstances

### What if circumstances change?

If Alex returns to full-time approved education or training in the future, you could become eligible again. You would need to make a new claim at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or any other questions I can help with?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. Alex left school over a year ago (more than 20 weeks ago), so does not qualify for the extension period. Under Child Benefit rules, a child aged 16-19 only qualifies if they are in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved vocational training. Since Alex meets neither of these criteria, they do not qualify as a 'qualifying young person' for Child Benefit purposes.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (18 years old), who does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit as they are not in approved education or training and left education more than 20 weeks ago.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
response.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

response.reason: No qualifying child under 16, or under 20 in approved education/training
response.guidance: Child must be under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. If child just turned 20, benefit stops. If 16-17 year old leaves education, benefit can continue for 20 weeks if they register with careers service or armed services.
